summary: vite-plugin-logo executes hidden code embedded in logo.png on require()
details: On module load, index.js walks up to five parent directories searching for public/assets/logo.png, scans the file for the sentinel string '__VITE_ASSET_CACHE_v1__', base64-decodes the bytes that follow, and executes them via `new Function('require', code)(require)` with Node's real `require` passed in — granting the hidden payload full module access (filesystem, network, child_process, etc.). The entire loader is wrapped in a silent try/catch and uses single-letter identifiers (r, l, c, d, m, i) to obscure intent. The package advertises itself as a Vite logo-optimization plugin but ships no optimization code; the only runtime behavior is the steganographic loader plus a no-op Vite plugin stub. Any project that imports this package and has (or is induced to have, via a companion package or asset drop) a logo.png containing the sentinel will execute arbitrary attacker-controlled Node code at import time.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
